title: Sudoku Backtracking Bug Fix: Repeated Column Values

statement.markdown:
Given a partially filled-in Sudoku grid, write a function to determine if it is possible to complete the grid according to the standard Sudoku rules.
A valid Sudoku grid is filled in according to the following rules: Each row, column, and 3x3 box contains the numbers 1-9 without duplication.

The grid is represented as a 2D array of integers
You must modify the existing function to make it work correctly in all test cases.

Note: This is a BUG FIX task. The existing function does not produce the expected output for all test cases.
statement.inputFormat: A 2D array of integers representing the Sudoku grid
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether a valid solution exists
statement.notes: The function should return True if a valid solution exists, False otherwise

examples.explanation: The Sudoku grid has a valid solution

hints.text: The existing function does not check for duplicate column values

editorial.title: Sudoku Backtracking Bug Fix: Repeated Column Values
editorial.intuition: A valid Sudoku grid has unique numbers in each row and column, but the existing function does not consider duplicate column values
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to try all possible solutions and check for validity
editorial.betterSolution: Use backtracking to find a valid solution
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a set to keep track of visited numbers in each row and column
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution checks for duplicate column values and returns False if found
editorial.dryRun: Create a sample Sudoku grid and run the function to see the output
commonMistakes: Not checking for duplicate column values
